steps.title: Match the variable terms
steps.content:
For a linear equation to have no solution, the variable terms must match while the constant terms differ. Match the coefficients of $x$:
$$-\frac{4}{21}r=-\frac{8}{63}$$

steps.title: Isolate the constant
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $-1$, then solve for $r$:
$$\frac{4}{21}r=\frac{8}{63}$$
$$r=\frac{8}{63}\cdot\frac{21}{4}=\frac{2}{3}$$
This value makes the two $x$-terms identical, so a different constant term can produce no solution. The answer is 2/3.
